text: Transport boundary is not validated by a trustworthy signal — auth method as transport class is 'which credential was used' not 'what environment this is'
Derived tools (resume, commitments, patterns, handoff, narrative) are the real leak surface — post-synthesis redaction is insufficient, must filter before synthesis
Visible redaction leaks more than threat model admits — namespace+key+tags+timestamps for clients/* reveals active client relationships
Namespace classification floors in code create a second policy system that will drift and fail open for new namespaces
Fail-closed is tool-by-tool manual enforcement — every tool must remember to do the right thing, which is not equivalent to actual fail-closed
Introduction of redacted entries is a breaking API contract change, not an additive change — existing consumers expect binary found/not-found
Audit/compliance evidence is overstated — session-ID fragmentation weakens per-session audit trails, and redaction log proves blocking not absence of leakage
Migration baseline too thin — no inventory of entries by proposed classification, especially projects/* which is heavily surfaced and likely contains client references
memory_query explain mode side-channels — ranking scores, distances, and reasons exposed for redacted entries
client-restricted depends on manual transport labeling for service tokens — admin misconfiguration can bypass the most sensitive classification level
redacted_sources in derived tools creates a new metadata leak surface — existence oracle for multiple protected entries at once
Persisted derivative rows (commitments table copies text/excerpts) not addressed — reclassification of source doesn't propagate to copied data
Mutable namespace_classification table is now a high-value target — single bad write lowers defaults for entire namespace family
Separate bearer tokens materially increase operational burden — rotation, provisioning, audit around which clients hold which credential
Partial-failure semantics for derived tools unspecified — what happens when one source in a multi-source tool has unknown classification
